title: How a Uniface Application Works
text: How a Uniface Application Works When developing Uniface applications it is important that you understand how Uniface processing works when the application is running so that you can influence application behavior appropriately. Uniface can run applications in several ways, and each approach uses specific types of components: Interactive desktop applications on Windows (GUI) or terminals (CHUI) use Form and Service components. For more information, see How a Desktop Application Works . Interactive web applications use Dynamic Server Page and Service components. For more information, see Uniface Web Applications . Interactive mobile applications on Android or iOS use Dynamic Server Page (DSP) and Service components. Static web application use Static Server Page (USP) and Service components. Non-interactive (batch) process on various server platforms use Service and Report components. Uniface Runtime Engine At the heart of a Uniface application is the Uniface Runtime Engine. It is responsible for instantiating components, interpreting user and system events, executing ProcScript, and communicating with the various types of connectors, such as a database connector and a network connector. The runtime engine is built into the Uniface Server and Uniface executables. Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
